Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress), and from classification under Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines and the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 for purposes of section 251 allocations, Committee on Appropriations 302(a) allocations, and Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act section 3(4)(C).
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 401. 
Budgetary effects
(a) 
Statutory PAYGO scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-
notes: Standard budget enforcement exemption provision for Division B (Extensions).
Subsection (a) exempts from Statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010.
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards under section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress).

Subsection (c) exempts from budget scorekeeping classification notwithstanding Rule 3 of the Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines and section 250(c)(8) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985, for three specific purposes: (1) section 251 discretionary spending limits, (2) 302(a) allocations to Appropriations Committee, and (3) Statutory PAYGO section 3(4)(C) classification as an appropriation Act.

flagged_issues: SEC. 101 CR baseline funds all accounts at FY2024 rates; amounts are indeterminate and not included in total_budget_authority
SEC. 145 appropriates 'such sums as may be necessary' for TANF continuation; indeterminate amount not included in total
SEC. 149 rescission and reappropriation amounts are equal and indeterminate (based on actual unobligated balances as of Sept 30, 2024); net zero budget impact

SEC. 131 contains two CR substitutions for OPM Salaries and Expenses: one reduces main amount by $28,292,000 (from $219,076,000 to $190,784,000); the other increases second proviso by $52,292,000 (from $192,975,000 to $245,267,000)
Rate-for-operations amounts in SEC. 126, 139, 142, 144 represent annualized rates; actual CR spending would be prorated for the Oct 1 - Dec 20 period

SEC. 136 contains significant obligation limitation: $231M in Secret Service funds may not be obligated until DHS Secretary transmits Mission Assurance Report related to assassination attempt on Donald J. Trump
SEC. 129 contains conditional future rescission of funds allotted to unsuccessful Presidential candidates
Division B Title I contains no appropriations — all provisions are authority extensions, policy amendments, reporting requirements, and limitations
Most provisions extend existing authorities through December 20, 2024 (the CR expiration date)
SEC. 105 contains both substantive policy changes (removing 'to the maximum extent practicable' qualifiers, adding reporting requirements) and a date extension, going beyond a simple sunset extension
SEC. 109(b) imposes a new two-thirds cap on locality-based comparability payments for Foreign Service members
SEC. 110 includes both gap-period continuation authority and structural amendments to the Compact of Free Association Amendments Act of 2024
SEC. 112 expands USAID IG annuitant waiver authority beyond simple extension to cover humanitarian disaster/complex emergency oversight
SEC. 222 sets Medicare Improvement Fund to $3,197,000,000 for FY2026, replacing a $0 balance — commonly used as a budget offset/pay-for vehicle
SEC. 211 provides $27,100,000 additional Medicaid funding for the Northern Mariana Islands for FY2023–FY2024, payable even if disbursed in FY2025
All mandatory_spending_extension amounts are excluded from total_budget_authority as they amend existing authorizing statutes rather than providing new discretionary appropriations
SEC. 334 authorizes $2,500,000 per year for FY2024-2027 for Paralympic/Olympic sports program; this is an authorization of appropriations, not a direct appropriation
SEC. 336 extends VA loan fee dates by only 14 days (November 15 to November 29, 2031), a common CBO scoring offset mechanism
SEC. 313 is a new policy authority for flat grave markers at Santa Fe National Cemetery rather than a date extension
SEC. 338 resets CFO requirement years from FY2022-2025 to FY2026-2029 rather than simply extending an end date
SEC. 324 amends two separate statutory provisions (Sections 2031(b) and 2033(d)) in subsections (a) and (b)
SEC. 401 is a standard PAYGO/budget enforcement exemption for Division B; no dollar amounts involved.
